message.content: Adversaries may exploit search engines to gather information about potential victims, leveraging the extensive indexing capabilities of these services. By crafting specific queries, they can uncover general details or sensitive data such as network information and credentials. This reconnaissance can facilitate further malicious activities, including phishing, account compromise, and establishing operational resources. For instance, the threat group Kimsuky has utilized Google to search for vulnerabilities and geopolitical trends to target victims. Mitigation strategies include monitoring for unusual search engine queries, educating users on the risks of information leakage, and implementing robust data protection measures to prevent sensitive information from being indexed by search engines.
